label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem. So there's a white elephant gift exchange with five people: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each starts with a different colored present. The question is asking what present Eve ends up with after a series of swaps. Let me go through each step carefully. First, let me note down the initial gifts: - Alice: yellow - Bob: orange ball - Claire: red - Dave: purple - Eve: green Now, the swaps happen in a specific order. Let's track each swap and see how the gifts move around. First swap: Alice and Eve swap. So Alice had yellow, Eve had green. After swapping, Alice has green, and Eve has yellow. Let me write that down. After first swap (Alice-Eve): - Alice: green - Eve: yellow Others remain the same for now: Bob (orange), Claire (red), Dave (purple). Second swap: Bob and Dave swap. Bob has orange ball, Dave has purple. After swapping, Bob gets purple, Dave gets orange. Let's update: After second swap (Bob-Dave): - Bob: purple - Dave: orange Others: Alice (green), Claire (red), Eve (yellow). Third swap: Bob and Eve swap. Now Bob has purple, Eve has yellow. After swapping, Bob gets yellow, Eve gets purple. After third swap (Bob-Eve): - Bob: yellow - Eve: purple Others: Alice (green), Claire (red), Dave (orange). Fourth swap: Claire and Eve swap. Claire has red, Eve has purple. After swap, Claire gets purple, Eve gets red. After fourth swap (Claire-Eve): - Claire: purple - Eve: red Others: Alice (green), Bob (yellow), Dave (orange). Fifth swap: Dave and Alice swap. Dave has orange, Alice has green. After swap, Dave gets green, Alice gets orange. After fifth swap (Dave-Alice): - Alice: orange - Dave: green Others: Bob (yellow), Claire (purple), Eve (red). So now, after all swaps, Eve has the red present. Let me check the
